members.name: _GetConnectionLine
members.summary: Virtual method which can be overridden to customize how connections are drawn.

members.name: _IsInInputHotzone
members.signature: public bool _IsInInputHotzone(Object inNode, int inPort, Vector2 mousePosition)
members.summary: Returns whether the [param mouse_position] is in the input hot zone. By default, a hot zone is a [Rect2] positioned such that its center is at [param in_node].[method GraphNode.get_input_port_position]([param in_port]) (For output's case, call [method GraphNode.get_output_port_position] instead). The hot zone's width is twice the Theme Property [code]port_grab_distance_horizontal[/code], and its height is twice the [code]port_grab_distance_vertical[/code]. Below is a sample code to help get started: [codeblock] func _is_in_input_hotzone(in_node, in_port, mouse_position): var port_size = Vector2(get_theme_constant("port_grab_distance_horizontal"), get_theme_constant("port_grab_distance_vertical")) var port_pos = in_node.get_position() + in_node.get_input_port_position(in_port) - port_size / 2 var rect = Rect2(port_pos, port_size) return rect.has_point(mouse_position) [/codeblock]

members.name: _IsInOutputHotzone
members.signature: public bool _IsInOutputHotzone(Object inNode, int inPort, Vector2 mousePosition)
members.summary: Returns whether the [param mouse_position] is in the output hot zone. For more information on hot zones, see [method _is_in_input_hotzone]. Below is a sample code to help get started: [codeblock] func _is_in_output_hotzone(in_node, in_port, mouse_position): var port_size = Vector2(get_theme_constant("port_grab_distance_horizontal"), get_theme_constant("port_grab_distance_vertical")) var port_pos = in_node.get_position() + in_node.get_output_port_position(in_port) - port_size / 2 var rect = Rect2(port_pos, port_size) return rect.has_point(mouse_position) [/codeblock]

members.name: _IsNodeHoverValid
members.signature: public bool _IsNodeHoverValid(StringName fromNode, int fromPort, StringName toNode, int toPort)
members.summary: This virtual method can be used to insert additional error detection while the user is dragging a connection over a valid port. Return [code]true[/code] if the connection is indeed valid or return [code]false[/code] if the connection is impossible. If the connection is impossible, no snapping to the port and thus no connection request to that port will happen. In this example a connection to same node is suppressed: [codeblocks] [gdscript] func _is_node_hover_valid(from, from_port, to, to_port): return from != to [/gdscript] [csharp] public override bool _IsNodeHoverValid(StringName fromNode, int fromPort, StringName toNode, int toPort) { return fromNode != toNode; } [/csharp] [/codeblocks]

members.name: AddValidConnectionType
members.signature: public void AddValidConnectionType(int fromType, int toType)
members.summary: Allows the connection between two different port types. The port type is defined individually for the left and the right port of each slot with the [method GraphNode.set_slot] method. See also [method is_valid_connection_type] and [method remove_valid_connection_type].

members.name: AddValidLeftDisconnectType
members.summary: Allows to disconnect nodes when dragging from the left port of the [GraphNode]'s slot if it has the specified type. See also [method remove_valid_left_disconnect_type].

members.name: AddValidRightDisconnectType
members.summary: Allows to disconnect nodes when dragging from the right port of the [GraphNode]'s slot if it has the specified type. See also [method remove_valid_right_disconnect_type].

members.name: ArrangeNodes
members.summary: Rearranges selected nodes in a layout with minimum crossings between connections and uniform horizontal and vertical gap between nodes.

members.name: AttachGraphElementToFrame
members.signature: public void AttachGraphElementToFrame(StringName element, StringName frame)
members.summary: Attaches the [param element] [GraphElement] to the [param frame] [GraphFrame].

members.name: ClearConnections
members.summary: Removes all connections between nodes.

members.name: ConnectNode
members.signature: public int ConnectNode(StringName fromNode, int fromPort, StringName toNode, int toPort, bool keepAlive)
members.summary: Create a connection between the [param from_port] of the [param from_node] [GraphNode] and the [param to_port] of the [param to_node] [GraphNode]. If the connection already exists, no connection is created. Connections with [param keep_alive] set to [code]false[/code] may be deleted automatically if invalid during a redraw.

members.name: DetachGraphElementFromFrame
members.summary: Detaches the [param element] [GraphElement] from the [GraphFrame] it is currently attached to.

members.name: DisconnectNode
members.signature: public void DisconnectNode(StringName fromNode, int fromPort, StringName toNode, int toPort)
members.summary: Removes the connection between the [param from_port] of the [param from_node] [GraphNode] and the [param to_port] of the [param to_node] [GraphNode]. If the connection does not exist, no connection is removed.

members.name: ForceConnectionDragEnd
members.summary: Ends the creation of the current connection. In other words, if you are dragging a connection you can use this method to abort the process and remove the line that followed your cursor. This is best used together with [signal connection_drag_started] and [signal connection_drag_ended] to add custom behavior like node addition through shortcuts. [b]Note:[/b] This method suppresses any other connection request signals apart from [signal connection_drag_ended].

members.name: GetAttachedNodesOfFrame
members.summary: Returns an array of node names that are attached to the [GraphFrame] with the given name.

members.name: GetClosestConnectionAtPoint
members.signature: public Dictionary GetClosestConnectionAtPoint(Vector2 point, float maxDistance)
members.summary: Returns the closest connection to the given point in screen space. If no connection is found within [param max_distance] pixels, an empty [Dictionary] is returned. A connection is represented as a [Dictionary] in the form of: [codeblock] { from_node: StringName, from_port: int, to_node: StringName, to_port: int, keep_alive: bool } [/codeblock] For example, getting a connection at a given mouse position can be achieved like this: [codeblocks] [gdscript] var connection = get_closest_connection_at_point(mouse_event.get_position()) [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

members.name: GetConnectionCount
members.signature: public int GetConnectionCount(StringName fromNode, int fromPort)
members.summary: Returns the number of connections from [param from_port] of [param from_node].

members.name: GetConnectionLine
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array GetConnectionLine(Vector2 fromNode, Vector2 toNode)
members.summary: Returns the points which would make up a connection between [param from_node] and [param to_node].

members.name: GetConnectionListFromNode
members.summary: Returns an [Array] containing a list of all connections for [param node]. A connection is represented as a [Dictionary] in the form of: [codeblock] { from_node: StringName, from_port: int, to_node: StringName, to_port: int, keep_alive: bool } [/codeblock] [b]Example:[/b] Get all connections on a specific port: [codeblock] func get_connection_list_from_port(node, port): var connections = get_connection_list_from_node(node) var result = [] for connection in connections: var dict = {} if connection["from_node"] == node and connection["from_port"] == port: dict["node"] = connection["to_node"] dict["port"] = connection["to_port"] dict["type"] = "left" result.push_back(dict) elif connection["to_node"] == node and connection["to_port"] == port: dict["node"] = connection["from_node"] dict["port"] = connection["from_port"] dict["type"] = "right" result.push_back(dict) return result [/codeblock]

members.name: GetConnectionsIntersectingWithRect
members.summary: Returns an [Array] containing the list of connections that intersect with the given [Rect2]. A connection is represented as a [Dictionary] in the form of: [codeblock] { from_node: StringName, from_port: int, to_node: StringName, to_port: int, keep_alive: bool } [/codeblock]

members.name: GetElementFrame
members.summary: Returns the [GraphFrame] that contains the [GraphElement] with the given name.

members.name: GetMenuHbox
members.summary: Gets the [HBoxContainer] that contains the zooming and grid snap controls in the top left of the graph. You can use this method to reposition the toolbar or to add your own custom controls to it. [b]Warning:[/b] This is a required internal node, removing and freeing it may cause a crash. If you wish to hide it or any of its children, use their [member CanvasItem.visible] property.

members.name: IsNodeConnected
members.signature: public bool IsNodeConnected(StringName fromNode, int fromPort, StringName toNode, int toPort)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the [param from_port] of the [param from_node] [GraphNode] is connected to the [param to_port] of the [param to_node] [GraphNode].

members.name: IsValidConnectionType
members.signature: public bool IsValidConnectionType(int fromType, int toType)
members.summary: Returns whether it's possible to make a connection between two different port types. The port type is defined individually for the left and the right port of each slot with the [method GraphNode.set_slot] method. See also [method add_valid_connection_type] and [method remove_valid_connection_type].

members.name: RemoveValidConnectionType
members.signature: public void RemoveValidConnectionType(int fromType, int toType)
members.summary: Disallows the connection between two different port types previously allowed by [method add_valid_connection_type]. The port type is defined individually for the left and the right port of each slot with the [method GraphNode.set_slot] method. See also [method is_valid_connection_type].

members.name: RemoveValidLeftDisconnectType
members.summary: Disallows to disconnect nodes when dragging from the left port of the [GraphNode]'s slot if it has the specified type. Use this to disable a disconnection previously allowed with [method add_valid_left_disconnect_type].

members.name: RemoveValidRightDisconnectType
members.summary: Disallows to disconnect nodes when dragging from the right port of the [GraphNode]'s slot if it has the specified type. Use this to disable a disconnection previously allowed with [method add_valid_right_disconnect_type].

members.name: SetConnectionActivity
members.signature: public void SetConnectionActivity(StringName fromNode, int fromPort, StringName toNode, int toPort, float amount)
members.summary: Sets the coloration of the connection between [param from_node]'s [param from_port] and [param to_node]'s [param to_port] with the color provided in the [theme_item activity] theme property. The color is linearly interpolated between the connection color and the activity color using [param amount] as weight.

members.name: SetSelected
members.summary: Sets the specified [param node] as the one selected.

members.name: ConnectionLinesAntialiased
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the lines between nodes will use antialiasing.

members.name: ConnectionLinesCurvature
members.summary: The curvature of the lines between the nodes. 0 results in straight lines.

members.name: ConnectionLinesThickness
members.summary: The thickness of the lines between the nodes.

members.name: Connections
members.summary: The connections between [GraphNode]s. A connection is represented as a [Dictionary] in the form of: [codeblock] { from_node: StringName, from_port: int, to_node: StringName, to_port: int, keep_alive: bool } [/codeblock] Connections with [code]keep_alive[/code] set to [code]false[/code] may be deleted automatically if invalid during a redraw.

members.name: GridPattern
members.summary: The pattern used for drawing the grid.

members.name: MinimapEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the minimap is visible.

members.name: MinimapOpacity
members.summary: The opacity of the minimap rectangle.

members.name: MinimapSize
members.summary: The size of the minimap rectangle. The map itself is based on the size of the grid area and is scaled to fit this rectangle.

members.name: PanningScheme
members.summary: Defines the control scheme for panning with mouse wheel.

members.name: RightDisconnects
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], enables disconnection of existing connections in the GraphEdit by dragging the right end.

members.name: ScrollOffset
members.summary: The scroll offset.

members.name: ShowArrangeButton
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the button to automatically arrange graph nodes is visible.

members.name: ShowGrid
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the grid is visible.

members.name: ShowGridButtons
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], buttons that allow to configure grid and snapping options are visible.

members.name: ShowMenu
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the menu toolbar is visible.

members.name: ShowMinimapButton
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the button to toggle the minimap is visible.

members.name: ShowZoomButtons
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], buttons that allow to change and reset the zoom level are visible.

members.name: ShowZoomLabel
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the label with the current zoom level is visible. The zoom level is displayed in percents.

members.name: SnappingDistance
members.summary: The snapping distance in pixels, also determines the grid line distance.

members.name: SnappingEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], enables snapping.

members.name: TypeNames
members.summary: [Dictionary] of human-readable port type names.

members.name: Zoom
members.summary: The current zoom value.

members.name: ZoomMax
members.summary: The upper zoom limit.

members.name: ZoomMin
members.summary: The lower zoom limit.

members.name: ZoomStep
members.summary: The step of each zoom level.

signals.name: BeginNodeMove
signals.summary: Emitted at the beginning of a [GraphElement]'s movement.

signals.name: ConnectionDragEnded
signals.summary: Emitted at the end of a connection drag.

signals.name: ConnectionDragStarted
signals.summary: Emitted at the beginning of a connection drag.

signals.name: ConnectionFromEmpty
signals.summary: Emitted when user drags a connection from an input port into the empty space of the graph.

signals.name: ConnectionRequest
signals.summary: Emitted to the GraphEdit when the connection between the [param from_port] of the [param from_node] [GraphNode] and the [param to_port] of the [param to_node] [GraphNode] is attempted to be created.

signals.name: ConnectionToEmpty
signals.summary: Emitted when user drags a connection from an output port into the empty space of the graph.

signals.name: CopyNodesRequest
signals.summary: Emitted when this [GraphEdit] captures a [code]ui_copy[/code] action ([kbd]Ctrl + C[/kbd] by default). In general, this signal indicates that the selected [GraphElement]s should be copied.

signals.name: CutNodesRequest
signals.summary: Emitted when this [GraphEdit] captures a [code]ui_cut[/code] action ([kbd]Ctrl + X[/kbd] by default). In general, this signal indicates that the selected [GraphElement]s should be cut.

signals.name: DeleteNodesRequest
signals.summary: Emitted when this [GraphEdit] captures a [code]ui_graph_delete[/code] action ([kbd]Delete[/kbd] by default). [param nodes] is an array of node names that should be removed. These usually include all selected nodes.

signals.name: DisconnectionRequest
signals.summary: Emitted to the GraphEdit when the connection between [param from_port] of [param from_node] [GraphNode] and [param to_port] of [param to_node] [GraphNode] is attempted to be removed.

signals.name: DuplicateNodesRequest
signals.summary: Emitted when this [GraphEdit] captures a [code]ui_graph_duplicate[/code] action ([kbd]Ctrl + D[/kbd] by default). In general, this signal indicates that the selected [GraphElement]s should be duplicated.

signals.name: EndNodeMove
signals.summary: Emitted at the end of a [GraphElement]'s movement.

signals.name: FrameRectChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the [GraphFrame] [param frame] is resized to [param new_rect].

signals.name: GraphElementsLinkedToFrameRequest
signals.summary: Emitted when one or more [GraphElement]s are dropped onto the [GraphFrame] named [param frame], when they were not previously attached to any other one. [param elements] is an array of [GraphElement]s to be attached.

signals.name: NodeDeselected
signals.summary: Emitted when the given [GraphElement] node is deselected.

signals.name: NodeSelected
signals.summary: Emitted when the given [GraphElement] node is selected.

signals.name: PasteNodesRequest
signals.summary: Emitted when this [GraphEdit] captures a [code]ui_paste[/code] action ([kbd]Ctrl + V[/kbd] by default). In general, this signal indicates that previously copied [GraphElement]s should be pasted.

signals.name: PopupRequest
signals.summary: Emitted when a popup is requested. Happens on right-clicking in the GraphEdit. [param at_position] is the position of the mouse pointer when the signal is sent.

signals.name: ScrollOffsetChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when the scroll offset is changed by the user. It will not be emitted when changed in code.

values.name: GRID_PATTERN_LINES
values.summary: Draw the grid using solid lines.

values.name: SCROLL_PANS
values.summary: [kbd]Mouse Wheel[/kbd] will move the view, [kbd]Ctrl + Mouse Wheel[/kbd] will zoom.

values.name: SCROLL_ZOOMS
values.summary: [kbd]Mouse Wheel[/kbd] will zoom, [kbd]Ctrl + Mouse Wheel[/kbd] will move the view.

constants.name: SCROLL_ZOOMS
constants.summary: [kbd]Mouse Wheel[/kbd] will zoom, [kbd]Ctrl + Mouse Wheel[/kbd] will move the view.

constants.name: SCROLL_PANS
constants.summary: [kbd]Mouse Wheel[/kbd] will move the view, [kbd]Ctrl + Mouse Wheel[/kbd] will zoom.

constants.name: GRID_PATTERN_LINES
constants.summary: Draw the grid using solid lines.

virtualMethods.name: _GetConnectionLine
virtualMethods.summary: Virtual method which can be overridden to customize how connections are drawn.

virtualMethods.name: _IsInInputHotzone
virtualMethods.signature: public bool _IsInInputHotzone(Object inNode, int inPort, Vector2 mousePosition)
virtualMethods.summary: Returns whether the [param mouse_position] is in the input hot zone. By default, a hot zone is a [Rect2] positioned such that its center is at [param in_node].[method GraphNode.get_input_port_position]([param in_port]) (For output's case, call [method GraphNode.get_output_port_position] instead). The hot zone's width is twice the Theme Property [code]port_grab_distance_horizontal[/code], and its height is twice the [code]port_grab_distance_vertical[/code]. Below is a sample code to help get started: [codeblock] func _is_in_input_hotzone(in_node, in_port, mouse_position): var port_size = Vector2(get_theme_constant("port_grab_distance_horizontal"), get_theme_constant("port_grab_distance_vertical")) var port_pos = in_node.get_position() + in_node.get_input_port_position(in_port) - port_size / 2 var rect = Rect2(port_pos, port_size) return rect.has_point(mouse_position) [/codeblock]

virtualMethods.name: _IsInOutputHotzone
virtualMethods.signature: public bool _IsInOutputHotzone(Object inNode, int inPort, Vector2 mousePosition)
virtualMethods.summary: Returns whether the [param mouse_position] is in the output hot zone. For more information on hot zones, see [method _is_in_input_hotzone]. Below is a sample code to help get started: [codeblock] func _is_in_output_hotzone(in_node, in_port, mouse_position): var port_size = Vector2(get_theme_constant("port_grab_distance_horizontal"), get_theme_constant("port_grab_distance_vertical")) var port_pos = in_node.get_position() + in_node.get_output_port_position(in_port) - port_size / 2 var rect = Rect2(port_pos, port_size) return rect.has_point(mouse_position) [/codeblock]

virtualMethods.name: _IsNodeHoverValid
virtualMethods.signature: public bool _IsNodeHoverValid(StringName fromNode, int fromPort, StringName toNode, int toPort)
virtualMethods.summary: This virtual method can be used to insert additional error detection while the user is dragging a connection over a valid port. Return [code]true[/code] if the connection is indeed valid or return [code]false[/code] if the connection is impossible. If the connection is impossible, no snapping to the port and thus no connection request to that port will happen. In this example a connection to same node is suppressed: [codeblocks] [gdscript] func _is_node_hover_valid(from, from_port, to, to_port): return from != to [/gdscript] [csharp] public override bool _IsNodeHoverValid(StringName fromNode, int fromPort, StringName toNode, int toPort) { return fromNode != toNode; } [/csharp] [/codeblocks]
